summary: The reproduction initializes the Pro route stack from commit 48ba499f83d521c4b42b69ea79770650c4959659, seeds a platform-user with no network roles, drives the public websocket registration endpoint with join_all=true, and verifies that the attacker-controlled host is added to both seeded networks.
expected_security_impact: A low-privilege platform-user with no network roles should be able to register an attacker-controlled host into every seeded network through WS /api/v1/auth-register/host.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned github.com/gravitl/netmaker at commit 48ba499f83d521c4b42b69ea79770650c4959659, built the harness image, executed the websocket registration flow, and produced EVOHUNT_RESULT output showing empty user_groups, empty user_network_roles, and joined_networks equal to victim-a and victim-b.
verification_method: reproduction/verify.sh runs the compiled harness, extracts the EVOHUNT_RESULT JSON line, and asserts that requested_join_all is true, the platform-user has no network roles or user groups, and the resulting host joined both seeded networks.
evidence_produced_by_run: The harness emitted EVOHUNT_RESULT JSON with joined_networks containing victim-a and victim-b.
The same result showed user_groups={} and user_network_roles={}, proving the joining user had no assigned network access.
The websocket flow completed against the real /api/v1/auth-register/host handler stack compiled from the pinned vulnerable commit.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, stages that checkout into the Docker build context, and mounts the host checkout into the container at /target-repo.
